nodes.name: nuts_recursive_tree_build
nodes.description: Recursively builds a binary tree for a No-U-Turn Sampler (NUTS) step. It takes an initial state and integration parameters, explores a trajectory using a provided leapfrog integrator, and selects a new state from the trajectory based on a slice variable and a U-turn condition. This represents the core computational kernel of a single NUTS transition.

inputs.name: direction_val
inputs.constraints: Determines the direction of integration, typically +1 for forward or -1 for backward.

inputs.name: step_size
inputs.constraints: The step size (epsilon) for the leapfrog integrator.

inputs.name: log_slice_variable
inputs.constraints: The logarithm of the uniform slice variable 'u', used for the generalized HMC acceptance criterion.

inputs.name: initial_hmc_state
inputs.constraints: The initial state for this subtree, containing position, momentum, potential energy (prev_U), and kinetic energy (prev_K).

inputs.name: log_prob_oracle
inputs.constraints: An oracle function (box_log_kernel_fn) that computes the log probability (potential energy) of the target distribution for a given position.

inputs.name: integrator_fn
inputs.constraints: The leapfrog integrator function (leap_frog_fn) used to propose new states along the trajectory.

inputs.name: tree_depth
inputs.constraints: The current recursion depth of the tree-building process.

outputs.name: trajectory_state
outputs.constraints: Returns a composite object representing the built trajectory, including the leftmost/rightmost states, the proposed sample, a flag indicating a U-turn, a divergence flag, and summed acceptance probabilities.

nodes.type_signature: (direction_val: integer, step_size: float, log_slice_variable: float, initial_hmc_state: HMCState, log_prob_oracle: Callable[[Position], float], integrator_fn: Callable[[State, float, int], State], tree_depth: integer) -> NUTS_Trajectory
nodes.conceptual_summary: recursive_trajectory_sampler. Recursively explores a trajectory within a high-dimensional state space defined by a scalar potential field (oracle). Using a volume-preserving state transition function, it doubles the search path at each step until a termination condition (e.g., path reversal) is met. A single state is then stochastically sampled from the generated trajectory, biased towards states with low potential.. Applications: Robotic motion planning: Efficiently finding valid, non-colliding configurations for a multi-joint arm by exploring the configuration space, where the potential field is defined by proximity to obstacles., Molecular conformation search: Discovering new stable or metastable structures of a protein by simulating its dynamics, where the state is atomic coordinates and the oracle is the molecular force field., Procedural content generation: Generating plausible and complex structures (e.g., terrains, networks) by exploring a design space, where the potential function encodes aesthetic or functional constraints., Inverse problem solving in geophysics: Inferring subsurface geological structures from surface measurements by sampling the high-dimensional space of possible models, where the oracle computes the misfit between simulated and observed data.

nodes.name: integrate_leaf_trajectory
nodes.description: In the recursion base case (tree_depth=0), performs a single leapfrog integration step. It then computes the potential (U) and kinetic (K) energies of the new state, checks if the state is within the valid energy slice (n_val), and determines if the simulation has become unstable (s_val). It also calculates the Metropolis-Hastings acceptance contribution.

inputs.name: direction_val
inputs.constraints: Specifies the integration direction (-1 or 1).

inputs.name: step_size
inputs.constraints: The size of each integration step.

inputs.name: log_rand_val
inputs.constraints: The logarithm of the uniform slice variable `u`.

inputs.name: initial_state
inputs.constraints: Contains the initial position (draw_vec), momentum (mntm_vec), potential energy (prev_U), and kinetic energy (prev_K).

inputs.name: inv_precond_matrix
inputs.constraints: Inverse of the preconditioning matrix (mass matrix).

inputs.name: box_log_kernel_fn
inputs.constraints: Function to compute the log probability and its gradient.

inputs.name: leap_frog_fn
inputs.constraints: The leapfrog integrator function.

inputs.name: target_data
inputs.constraints: Auxiliary data for kernel and integrator functions.

outputs.name: leaf_trajectory_result
outputs.constraints: Contains the new state (draw/momentum), trajectory endpoints, slice sample count (n_val), stop flag (s_val), and acceptance info (alpha_val, n_alpha_val).

nodes.type_signature: (direction_val: integer, step_size: fp_t, log_rand_val: fp_t, initial_state: HMCState, inv_precond_matrix: Mat_t, box_log_kernel_fn: Callable, leap_frog_fn: Callable, target_data: void*) -> NUTS_Trajectory_Leaf

nodes.name: build_initial_subtree
nodes.description: Performs the first recursive call to `nuts_build_tree` with a decremented tree depth to build the initial half of the trajectory.

inputs.name: initial_state
inputs.constraints: The state at the beginning of the current tree level.

inputs.name: tree_depth
inputs.constraints: Current recursion depth, will be decremented for the call.

outputs.name: subtree1_result
outputs.constraints: The result of the first sub-tree build, containing its proposed state and statistics.

nodes.type_signature: (direction_val: integer, step_size: fp_t, log_rand_val: fp_t, initial_state: HMCState, inv_precond_matrix: Mat_t, box_log_kernel_fn: Callable, leap_frog_fn: Callable, tree_depth: size_t, rand_engine: rand_engine_t, target_data: void*) -> NUTS_Trajectory_Recursive

nodes.name: build_doubled_subtree
nodes.description: If the initial subtree is valid, performs a second recursive call to `nuts_build_tree` to double the trajectory. The starting point for this build is the endpoint of the first subtree, in the specified direction.

inputs.name: initial_state
inputs.constraints: The state at the beginning of the current tree level.

inputs.name: subtree1_endpoints
inputs.constraints: The position/momentum at the positive and negative ends of the first subtree.

inputs.name: tree_depth
inputs.constraints: Current recursion depth, will be decremented for the call.

outputs.name: subtree2_result
outputs.constraints: The result of the second sub-tree build.

nodes.type_signature: (direction_val: integer, step_size: fp_t, log_rand_val: fp_t, initial_state: HMCState, subtree1_endpoints: TrajectoryEndpoints, inv_precond_matrix: Mat_t, box_log_kernel_fn: Callable, leap_frog_fn: Callable, tree_depth: size_t, rand_engine: rand_engine_t, target_data: void*) -> NUTS_Trajectory_Recursive

nodes.name: combine_and_sample_proposals
nodes.description: Probabilistically selects a new proposed state from the two subtrees, weighted by the number of valid samples in each. It also aggregates the statistics (sample count, alpha) from both subtrees.

inputs.name: proposal1
inputs.constraints: The proposed state from the first subtree.

inputs.name: stats1
inputs.constraints: Statistics (n_val, alpha_val, n_alpha_val) from the first subtree.

inputs.name: proposal2
inputs.constraints: The proposed state from the second subtree.

inputs.name: stats2
inputs.constraints: Statistics from the second subtree.

inputs.name: rand_engine
inputs.constraints: Used for the probabilistic selection.

outputs.name: selected_proposal
outputs.constraints: The newly chosen state for this level of the tree.

outputs.name: aggregated_stats
outputs.constraints: The combined statistics from both subtrees.

nodes.type_signature: (proposal1: ColVec_t, stats1: TrajectoryStats, proposal2: ColVec_t, stats2: TrajectoryStats, rand_engine: rand_engine_t) -> tuple[ColVec_t, TrajectoryStats]

nodes.name: check_global_u_turn
nodes.description: Checks the No-U-Turn condition across the entire combined trajectory using the outermost endpoints. This determines if the tree should stop growing in the next iteration.

inputs.name: combined_trajectory_endpoints
inputs.constraints: The position/momentum at the far positive and negative ends of the full trajectory.

inputs.name: subtree2_stop_flag
inputs.constraints: The stop flag from the second, most recently built subtree.

outputs.name: global_stop_flag
outputs.constraints: A flag (0 or 1) indicating if a U-turn was detected and the trajectory should stop.
